mnemonics: RGB NOISE: Red, Green, Blue - Not One, but Several grains (noise types)
keyPoints: Color images have noise in each RGB channel independently
Noise can be correlated (like chrominance noise) or uncorrelated (like luminance noise)
Noise reduction must preserve color relationships to avoid artifacts
commonMistakes: Treating noise equally across all color channels
Applying grayscale denoising techniques directly to color images
Over-smoothing that causes loss of color edges and detail
